title: Revival of S. elongatus from plates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eo3uh","text":"On receiving wild type or tranformed S. elongatus plates, one needs to revive them for further studies. Revival of S. elongatus involves making its liquid culture in BG-11 medium. The following protocol describes the liquid culture preparation from BG-11 plates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Take a 50 mL autoclaved flask. Remove the foil on the flask for aeration purposes.
Take a 10 mL aliquot of BG-11 solution in a falcon tube and transfer it into the flask. Keep the flask aside Carry out this process and all the successive steps inside a laminar airflow hood
Dip the pipette tip in the aliquot of the BG-11 medium and gently mix. To mix, gently resuspend the liquid 1-2 times.

Take the cyanobacteria plate and gently scrape it with the tip of a 1 mL loaded micropipette. Position the plate such that the streaked part faces the roof of the laminar hood, remove the lid carefully and place it upside down.
For the engineered strains, apply appropriate antibiotics (10μg/mL kanamycin and 4μg/mL gentamicin) in the BG-11 medium.
Store all the flasks in a photo incubator at 15000 lux, 150 rpm, 37 °C,
creator: Akashdutta
